hypothesis: Integrating an uncertainty-aware, multi-database physics feedback into Direct Preference Optimization (DPO) by computing per-candidate confidence from an ensemble of CALPHAD databases and penalizing high-uncertainty predictions in the reward will stabilize DPO training for small LMs (e.g., OLMo), reduce out-of-distribution drift, and simultaneously improve BCC/B2 space coverage and novelty while maintaining compositional validity.
